stories.title: 卖掉旧账号
stories.source_range: inspired by early family pressure and account-selling scenes

sentences.chinese: 李贤家里一直没有很多钱。
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun's family has not had much money for a long time.

sentences.chinese: 白天，他在加油站工作。
sentences.english_helper: During the day, he works at a gas station.

sentences.chinese: 晚上，他回家以后，还要照顾奶奶和妹妹。
sentences.english_helper: At night, after he goes home, he still has to take care of his grandma and younger sister.

sentences.chinese: 奶奶身体不好，最近常常生病。
sentences.english_helper: His grandma is not in good health and has often been sick lately.

sentences.chinese: 妹妹虽然不说，可是李贤看得出来，她每天都在担心家里的钱。
sentences.english_helper: His younger sister does not say it, but Lee Hyun can tell that she worries about the family's money every day.

sentences.chinese: 李贤自己也很急，可是他不喜欢把“我很难”放在脸上。
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun is worried too, but he does not like to show how hard things are on his face.

sentences.chinese: 他总是说“没事”，然后一个人想办法。
sentences.english_helper: He always says, 'It's okay,' and then thinks of a solution by himself.

sentences.chinese: 他还有一个旧游戏账号。
sentences.english_helper: He still has an old game account.

sentences.chinese: 那个账号陪了他很久。
sentences.english_helper: That account has been with him for a long time.

sentences.chinese: 以前他最难过的时候，他常常打开电脑，看着那个账号，心里会安静一点。
sentences.english_helper: When he felt the saddest before, he often opened the computer and felt a little calmer looking at that account.

sentences.chinese: 所以在他心里，那个账号不只是钱，也是他最后一点希望。
sentences.english_helper: So in his heart, that account is not only money, but also his last little bit of hope.

sentences.chinese: 他一直告诉自己，只要这个账号还在，他就不算什么都没有。
sentences.english_helper: He keeps telling himself that as long as the account still exists, he does not count as having nothing.

sentences.chinese: 可是有一天晚上，奶奶又病了。
sentences.english_helper: But one night, his grandma gets sick again.

sentences.chinese: 妹妹小声问他：“明天买药的钱在哪里？”
sentences.english_helper: His younger sister softly asks, 'Where is the money for tomorrow's medicine?'

sentences.chinese: 李贤没有马上回答。
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun does not answer right away.

sentences.chinese: 他看着桌子上的卡，又看着房间里的奶奶，心里很乱。
sentences.english_helper: He looks at the card on the table, then at his grandma in the room, and feels very mixed up inside.

sentences.chinese: 一个声音说：“不能卖。
sentences.english_helper: One voice says, 'You cannot sell it.'

sentences.chinese: 卖了以后，你就真的什么都没有了。”
sentences.english_helper: It says, 'If you sell it, then you really will have nothing left.'

sentences.chinese: 另一个声音说：“如果不卖，家里怎么办？”
sentences.english_helper: Another voice says, 'If you do not sell it, what will happen to the family?'

sentences.chinese: 他坐了很久，才慢慢明白，自己一直舍不得的不是游戏本身，而是那个“我还没有输”的感觉。
sentences.english_helper: He sits for a long time and slowly understands that what he cannot let go of is not the game itself, but the feeling that he has not lost yet.

sentences.chinese: 第二天，他去把旧账号卖了，把钱带回家。
sentences.english_helper: The next day, he goes to sell the old account and brings the money home.

sentences.chinese: 奶奶先愣了一下，然后轻轻笑了。
sentences.english_helper: His grandma freezes for a moment first, then smiles softly.

sentences.chinese: 妹妹也终于松了一口气。
sentences.english_helper: His younger sister finally feels relieved too.

sentences.chinese: 李贤心里还是有点空，可是那种空没有让他更怕。
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun still feels a little empty, but that emptiness does not make him more afraid.

sentences.chinese: 相反，他第一次觉得自己更稳了。
sentences.english_helper: Instead, for the first time, he feels steadier.

sentences.chinese: 他知道，真正让人安心的，不是手里留着什么东西，而是自己愿意为了家里去做对的事。
sentences.english_helper: He knows that what truly makes a person feel calm is not keeping something in your hand, but being willing to do the right thing for your family.

stories.title: 不想让妹妹看见的手
stories.source_range: inspired by early work hardship and family scenes

sentences.chinese: 那天晚上，李贤比平常更晚回家。
sentences.english_helper: That night, Lee Hyun comes home later than usual.

sentences.chinese: 他一开门，就先把手放到背后。
sentences.english_helper: As soon as he opens the door, he puts his hands behind his back.

sentences.chinese: 妹妹马上看了他一眼。
sentences.english_helper: His younger sister looks at him right away.

sentences.chinese: 李贤笑着说：“我回来了。”
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun smiles and says, 'I'm home.'

sentences.chinese: 妹妹没有笑。
sentences.english_helper: His younger sister does not smile.

sentences.chinese: 她只问：“今天很累吗？”
sentences.english_helper: She only asks, 'Was today very tiring?'

sentences.chinese: 李贤还是说：“没事。”
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun still says, 'It's okay.'

sentences.chinese: 其实他今天搬了很多东西。
sentences.english_helper: Actually, he carried many things today.

sentences.chinese: 手上有两处小小的擦伤。
sentences.english_helper: There are two small scrapes on his hands.

sentences.chinese: 右手还有一个新的伤口。
sentences.english_helper: There is also a new wound on his right hand.

sentences.chinese: 刚才回来的路上，他一直在想，不能让妹妹看见。
sentences.english_helper: On the way home, he keeps thinking that he cannot let his sister see it.

sentences.chinese: 在他心里，家里已经很难了。
sentences.english_helper: In his mind, home life is already hard enough.

sentences.chinese: 如果连他也看起来很累，妹妹一定会更怕。
sentences.english_helper: If even he looks worn out, his sister will surely be more afraid.

sentences.chinese: 所以他一直有一个习惯。
sentences.english_helper: So he has one habit.

sentences.chinese: 不管多痛，都先说“没事”。
sentences.english_helper: No matter how much it hurts, he first says, 'It's okay.'

sentences.chinese: 妹妹去拿水的时候，李贤想快点回房间。
sentences.english_helper: When his sister goes to get water, Lee Hyun wants to hurry to his room.

sentences.chinese: 可是他一转身，手还是碰到了门边。
sentences.english_helper: But when he turns around, his hand still bumps the side of the door.

sentences.chinese: 他马上吸了一口气。
sentences.english_helper: He immediately sucks in a breath.

sentences.chinese: 妹妹听见声音，立刻走了过来。
sentences.english_helper: His younger sister hears the sound and comes over at once.

sentences.chinese: 她看见他的手，脸一下子白了。
sentences.english_helper: She sees his hand, and her face goes pale at once.

sentences.chinese: 她小声说：“哥哥，你为什么不早说？”
sentences.english_helper: She softly says, 'Brother, why didn't you say so earlier?'

sentences.chinese: 李贤本来还想笑一下。
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun still wants to smile it off.

sentences.chinese: 可是他看见妹妹的眼睛，突然说不出话。
sentences.english_helper: But when he sees his sister's eyes, he suddenly cannot speak.

sentences.chinese: 他第一次想到，也许自己一直这样逞强，并没有让妹妹放心。
sentences.english_helper: For the first time, he thinks that maybe acting tough like this has not actually reassured his sister.

sentences.chinese: 也许妹妹最怕的，不是伤口。
sentences.english_helper: Maybe what she fears most is not the wound.

sentences.chinese: 妹妹最怕的是，哥哥什么都不说。
sentences.english_helper: What she fears most is that her brother says nothing.

sentences.chinese: 李贤慢慢坐下，把手伸出来。
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun slowly sits down and holds out his hand.

sentences.chinese: 他说：“今天真的有一点痛。”
sentences.english_helper: He says, 'Today it really does hurt a little.'

sentences.chinese: 妹妹没有哭。
sentences.english_helper: His younger sister does not cry.

sentences.chinese: 她只是去拿药，又认真帮他包扎。
sentences.english_helper: She only goes to get medicine and carefully bandages him.

sentences.chinese: 包扎完以后，她说：“下次早点告诉我。”
sentences.english_helper: After bandaging him, she says, 'Tell me earlier next time.'

sentences.chinese: 李贤点了点头。
sentences.english_helper: Lee Hyun nods.

sentences.chinese: 那天晚上，他还是很累。
sentences.english_helper: That night, he is still very tired.

sentences.chinese: 可是他的心比平常更安静。
sentences.english_helper: But his heart is quieter than usual.

sentences.chinese: 他终于明白，保护家里的人，不一定是什么都自己藏起来。
sentences.english_helper: He finally understands that protecting your family does not always mean hiding everything by yourself.

stories.title: 一直打稻草人的人
stories.source_range: inspired by early training hall scenes

sentences.chinese: 杂草进入皇家之路以后，没有先去找热闹的地方，也没有急着去做简单任务。
sentences.english_helper: After Weed enters Royal Road, he does not first look for lively places and does not hurry to do easy quests.

sentences.chinese: 他一到塞拉堡城，就先走进训练场。
sentences.english_helper: As soon as he arrives in Serabourg, he first walks into the training hall.

sentences.chinese: 别的新手看了一圈，打几下训练稻草人，很快就走了。
sentences.english_helper: Other beginners look around, hit the training scarecrow a few times, and leave quickly.

sentences.chinese: 有人去市场，有人去找队友，还有人笑杂草。
sentences.english_helper: Some people go to the market, some go to find teammates, and some laugh at Weed.

sentences.chinese: 他们问：“你一直打这个木头人，要打到什么时候？”
sentences.english_helper: They ask, 'You keep hitting this wooden man. How long are you going to do that?'

sentences.chinese: 杂草没有回话，只是继续拿着木剑，一下一下地打。
sentences.english_helper: Weed does not answer. He just keeps holding the wooden sword and hitting again and again.

sentences.chinese: 在别人眼里，这很奇怪。
sentences.english_helper: In other people's eyes, this is very strange.

sentences.chinese: 在杂草心里，这却很重要。
sentences.english_helper: But in Weed's heart, this is very important.

sentences.chinese: 他一直有一个想法：自己开始得太晚，钱也不多，运气也不会特别好。
sentences.english_helper: He keeps thinking that he started too late, does not have much money, and will not have especially good luck.

sentences.chinese: 如果不比别人更苦、更久、更能忍，他以后一定还是弱的人。
sentences.english_helper: If he is not more enduring and works longer than others, he will still be weak later.

sentences.chinese: 所以他不肯停。
sentences.english_helper: So he refuses to stop.

sentences.chinese: 手痛了，他继续。
sentences.english_helper: His hands hurt, and he continues.

sentences.chinese: 肚子饿了，他继续。
sentences.english_helper: He gets hungry, and he continues.

sentences.chinese: 天黑了，他还是继续。
sentences.english_helper: It gets dark, and he still continues.

sentences.chinese: 可是打得越久，他心里越乱。
sentences.english_helper: But the longer he keeps hitting, the more confused he feels inside.

sentences.chinese: 他开始想：“我是不是太慢了？”
sentences.english_helper: He starts to think, 'Am I too slow?'

sentences.chinese: 他又想：“我是不是再练也没有用？”
sentences.english_helper: He also thinks, 'Even if I keep training, is it useless?'

sentences.chinese: 教官一直在旁边看。
sentences.english_helper: The instructor keeps watching from the side.

sentences.chinese: 教官没有马上说话。
sentences.english_helper: The instructor does not speak right away.

sentences.chinese: 只在杂草快没有力气的时候，教官问了一句：“你是在练，还是只是在生气？”
sentences.english_helper: Only when Weed is nearly out of strength does the instructor ask, 'Are you training, or are you only being angry?'

sentences.chinese: 这句话让杂草愣住了。
sentences.english_helper: That sentence stuns Weed.

sentences.chinese: 他第一次停下来，看着手里的木剑，又看着前面的稻草人。
sentences.english_helper: For the first time, he stops and looks at the wooden sword in his hand, then at the scarecrow in front of him.

sentences.chinese: 杂草突然明白，一直乱打，不等于真正变强。
sentences.english_helper: Weed suddenly understands that wildly hitting all the time does not mean truly becoming stronger.

sentences.chinese: 他不是在跟稻草人练，而是在跟自己心里的怕打。
sentences.english_helper: He is not really training against the scarecrow. He is fighting the fear inside himself.

sentences.chinese: 从那以后，杂草还是继续练，可是方法变了。
sentences.english_helper: After that, Weed still keeps training, but his method changes.

sentences.chinese: 他先看，再想，然后再打。
sentences.english_helper: He first looks, then thinks, and only then hits.

sentences.chinese: 每一下都更稳，也更安静。
sentences.english_helper: Each strike is steadier and calmer.

sentences.chinese: 没过多久，教官走过来，把一个特别任务交给了他。
sentences.english_helper: Not long later, the instructor walks over and gives him a special quest.

sentences.chinese: 那一刻，杂草才知道，真正让人变强的，不只是吃苦，也是愿意改自己。
sentences.english_helper: At that moment, Weed finally knows that what truly makes a person strong is not only enduring hardship, but also being willing to change yourself.

stories.title: 训练场最后一个人
stories.source_range: inspired by early training repetition and instructor scenes

sentences.chinese: 那一天，训练场里的人一个一个走了。
sentences.english_helper: That day, the people in the training yard leave one by one.

sentences.chinese: 有人去吃饭，有人去接任务。
sentences.english_helper: Some go to eat, and some go to take quests.

sentences.chinese: 最后只剩下杂草。
sentences.english_helper: In the end, only Weed is left.

sentences.chinese: 他站在木头人前面，反复练同一个动作。
sentences.english_helper: He stands in front of the wooden dummy and repeats the same movement.

sentences.chinese: 可是练了很久，还是不对。
sentences.english_helper: But even after practicing for a long time, it is still not right.

sentences.chinese: 木剑总是偏一点。
sentences.english_helper: The wooden sword is always a little off.

sentences.chinese: 脚也总是慢半步。
sentences.english_helper: His foot is also always half a beat too slow.

sentences.chinese: 教官在远处看着，没有马上过来。
sentences.english_helper: The instructor watches from far away and does not come over immediately.

sentences.chinese: 杂草心里很急。
sentences.english_helper: Weed feels very anxious inside.

sentences.chinese: 他知道自己做得不对。
sentences.english_helper: He knows he is doing it wrong.

sentences.chinese: 可是他不想问。
sentences.english_helper: But he does not want to ask.

sentences.chinese: 在他心里，自己如果连这种事都要问，说明自己太弱。
sentences.english_helper: In his mind, if he has to ask even about this, it means he is too weak.

sentences.chinese: 所以他只是一直重复。
sentences.english_helper: So he only keeps repeating it.

sentences.chinese: 一次不行，就再来一次。
sentences.english_helper: If it does not work once, he tries again.

sentences.chinese: 十次不行，就再来十次。
sentences.english_helper: If it does not work ten times, he does ten more.

sentences.chinese: 可是越这样，他的身体越硬。
sentences.english_helper: But the more he does this, the stiffer his body becomes.

sentences.chinese: 心里也越烦。
sentences.english_helper: His mind also becomes more irritated.

sentences.chinese: 后来，教官终于走过来。
sentences.english_helper: Later, the instructor finally walks over.

sentences.chinese: 教官没有笑他。
sentences.english_helper: The instructor does not laugh at him.

sentences.chinese: 教官只是说：“你的手太紧了。”
sentences.english_helper: The instructor only says, 'Your hand is too tight.'

sentences.chinese: 杂草听见以后，脸一下子热了。
sentences.english_helper: After hearing that, Weed's face suddenly feels hot.

sentences.chinese: 因为这句话很简单。
sentences.english_helper: Because the sentence is very simple.

sentences.chinese: 简单得像他早一点开口就能得到。
sentences.english_helper: It is so simple that he could have gotten it earlier if he had spoken up.

sentences.chinese: 教官又提醒他说：“不是一直用力，就会更强。”
sentences.english_helper: The instructor reminds him again, 'Using force all the time will not make you stronger.'

sentences.chinese: “合适的时候放松，动作才会对。”
sentences.english_helper: 'Only when you relax at the right time will the movement be correct.'

sentences.chinese: 杂草这一次没有装作没听见。
sentences.english_helper: This time, Weed does not pretend he did not hear it.

sentences.chinese: 他低声问：“那我应该先看哪里？”
sentences.english_helper: He quietly asks, 'Then where should I look first?'

sentences.chinese: 教官指了指他的手，又指了指他的脚。
sentences.english_helper: The instructor points at his hand and then at his foot.

sentences.chinese: 杂草再练一次的时候，真的比刚才顺了很多。
sentences.english_helper: When Weed practices again, it really feels much smoother than before.

sentences.chinese: 那一刻他才明白，自己刚才不是在坚持。
sentences.english_helper: At that moment, he finally understands that what he was doing before was not real perseverance.

sentences.chinese: 自己刚才只是不肯承认，别人也能帮他变强。
sentences.english_helper: Before, he simply refused to admit that other people could also help him become stronger.

sentences.chinese: 训练场里还是只有他一个人。
sentences.english_helper: There is still only him in the training yard.

sentences.chinese: 可是这一次，他不再觉得一个人就一定要什么都自己扛。
sentences.english_helper: But this time, he no longer feels that being alone means carrying everything by himself.

stories.title: 不想听故事的人
stories.target_level_note: low-level discovery story with a simple internal shift

sentences.chinese: 杂草为了一个关于月光的任务，先去找雕刻店的店主，又去酒馆找吟游诗人，后来还去找旧女仆。
sentences.english_helper: For a quest about moonlight, Weed first goes to the sculpture shop keeper, then to the tavern bard, and later to the old maid.

sentences.chinese: 每个人都只说一点点，没有一个人把话一次说完。
sentences.english_helper: Each person says only a little. No one says everything at once.

sentences.chinese: 杂草本来就不是喜欢听故事的人。
sentences.english_helper: Weed was never a person who liked listening to stories.

sentences.chinese: 他觉得故事太慢，也太远。
sentences.english_helper: He feels stories are too slow and too far away.

sentences.chinese: 故事不能马上变成钱，也不能马上让人变强。
sentences.english_helper: Stories cannot immediately turn into money, and they cannot immediately make a person stronger.

sentences.chinese: 所以他一开始只想快点听完，快点离开。
sentences.english_helper: So at first he only wants to hear it quickly and leave quickly.

sentences.chinese: 可是事情没有那么简单。
sentences.english_helper: But things are not that simple.

sentences.chinese: 店主只告诉他王宫里有一个旧传说。
sentences.english_helper: The shopkeeper only tells him that there is an old legend in the royal palace.

sentences.chinese: 吟游诗人只唱了几句歌。
sentences.english_helper: The bard sings only a few lines.

sentences.chinese: 旧女仆又一直说从前的事。
sentences.english_helper: The old maid keeps talking about things from the past.

sentences.chinese: 杂草越听越烦，差一点就想走。
sentences.english_helper: The more Weed listens, the more annoyed he gets, and he almost wants to leave.

sentences.chinese: 他心里一直有一个想法：只有马上有用的东西，才值得花时间。
sentences.english_helper: He keeps thinking that only things that are useful right away are worth spending time on.

sentences.chinese: 听这些旧话，太浪费时间了。
sentences.english_helper: Listening to all these old words is too much of a waste of time.

sentences.chinese: 但当他真的站起来准备离开时，他忽然想到自己在训练场的日子。
sentences.english_helper: But when he really stands up to leave, he suddenly thinks about his days in the training hall.

sentences.chinese: 那时候，别人也觉得打稻草人没有用。
sentences.english_helper: At that time, other people also thought hitting the scarecrow was useless.

sentences.chinese: 可是正是那些看起来没用的时间，让他后来拿到了任务。
sentences.english_helper: But it was exactly that seemingly useless time that later got him the quest.

sentences.chinese: 想到这里，他又坐下来了。
sentences.english_helper: When he thinks of this, he sits back down.

sentences.chinese: 这一次，他不再只想“快点结束”，而是开始认真听，认真问，也认真把每个人的话放在一起想。
sentences.english_helper: This time, he no longer only wants it to end quickly. He starts to listen carefully, ask carefully, and think carefully about everyone's words together.

sentences.chinese: 慢慢地，一条线在他眼前清楚起来。
sentences.english_helper: Slowly, one line becomes clear in front of him.

sentences.chinese: 月光不是普通的名字，扎哈布也不是普通的雕刻家。
sentences.english_helper: Moonlight is not an ordinary name, and Zahab is not an ordinary sculptor.

sentences.chinese: 那份约定、那个夜晚、那把像剑一样的雕刻刀，原来都连在一起。
sentences.english_helper: That promise, that night, and that sculpting knife like a sword all turn out to be connected.

sentences.chinese: 最后，杂草得到了月光雕刻师的路。
sentences.english_helper: In the end, Weed finds the path of the Moonlight Sculptor.

sentences.chinese: 以前的他也许会因为怕麻烦而转身就走，这一次他却没有。
sentences.english_helper: In the past, he might have turned around and left because he feared trouble, but this time he does not.

sentences.chinese: 他终于明白，有些最好的东西，不会大声叫你过去。
sentences.english_helper: He finally understands that some of the best things do not loudly call you over.

sentences.chinese: 你要先安静下来，才会真的听见。
sentences.english_helper: You have to become quiet first before you can really hear.

stories.title: 最后一句旧话
stories.source_range: inspired by old-servant clue scenes and late revelations

sentences.chinese: 杂草找到旧女仆的时候，天已经不早了。
sentences.english_helper: By the time Weed finds the old maid, it is already getting late.

sentences.chinese: 旧女仆走得很慢，说话也很慢。
sentences.english_helper: The old maid walks slowly and also speaks slowly.

sentences.chinese: 她先说王宫的花。
sentences.english_helper: She first talks about the flowers in the palace.

sentences.chinese: 后来又说以前的天气。
sentences.english_helper: Later she talks about the weather in the past.

sentences.chinese: 再后来，她开始说一个很久以前的晚上。
sentences.english_helper: After that, she starts talking about a night from long ago.

sentences.chinese: 杂草一开始还认真听。
sentences.english_helper: At first, Weed listens seriously.

sentences.chinese: 可是听了一会儿以后，他心里又急了。
sentences.english_helper: But after listening for a while, he becomes impatient again.

sentences.chinese: 他觉得真正的线索一定早就说完了。
sentences.english_helper: He feels that the real clue must already have been said.

sentences.chinese: 后面这些旧话，只是在拖时间。
sentences.english_helper: These old words afterward are only dragging out time.

sentences.chinese: 他站起来一次。
sentences.english_helper: He stands up once.

sentences.chinese: 可是旧女仆没有看他。
sentences.english_helper: But the old maid does not look at him.

sentences.chinese: 她还是慢慢往下说。
sentences.english_helper: She simply keeps talking slowly.

sentences.chinese: 杂草又坐下。
sentences.english_helper: Weed sits down again.

sentences.chinese: 他告诉自己，再听一点。
sentences.english_helper: He tells himself to listen a little longer.

sentences.chinese: 可是再过一会儿，他又差不多想走。
sentences.english_helper: But after a little more time, he almost wants to leave again.

sentences.chinese: 在他心里，耐心一直不是很有用的东西。
sentences.english_helper: In his mind, patience has never been a very useful thing.

sentences.chinese: 他更相信快一点、准一点。
sentences.english_helper: He trusts speed and accuracy more.

sentences.chinese: 可就在他准备第二次站起来的时候，旧女仆忽然提到了一把刀。
sentences.english_helper: But just as he gets ready to stand up a second time, the old maid suddenly mentions a knife.

sentences.chinese: 那不是普通的刀。
sentences.english_helper: It is not an ordinary knife.

sentences.chinese: 那把刀只在月亮很亮的晚上出现。
sentences.english_helper: That knife appears only on nights when the moon is bright.

sentences.chinese: 杂草一下子坐直了。
sentences.english_helper: Weed suddenly sits up straight.

sentences.chinese: 旧女仆说得还是很慢。
sentences.english_helper: The old maid is still speaking slowly.

sentences.chinese: 可是杂草这一次一句也不敢漏。
sentences.english_helper: But this time, Weed does not dare miss a single sentence.

sentences.chinese: 他甚至开始把几个重要的词记下。
sentences.english_helper: He even begins to write down a few important words.

sentences.chinese: 旧女仆最后说完的时候，天已经黑了。
sentences.english_helper: By the time the old maid finishes, it is already dark.

sentences.chinese: 杂草却一点也不觉得浪费。
sentences.english_helper: But Weed does not feel that it was a waste at all.

sentences.chinese: 他终于明白，重要的话不一定会在最前面。
sentences.english_helper: He finally understands that important words do not always come first.

sentences.chinese: 有时候，最关键的一句会躲在最后。
sentences.english_helper: Sometimes the most important sentence hides at the end.

sentences.chinese: 他离开的时候，脚步比来的时候更快。
sentences.english_helper: When he leaves, his steps are quicker than when he arrived.

sentences.chinese: 可是他的心，比刚来的时候更稳。
sentences.english_helper: But his heart is steadier than when he first came.

stories.title: 看起来没用的职业
stories.source_range: inspired by early class-choice and sculptor-path scenes

sentences.chinese: 杂草终于走到了可以换职业的时候。
sentences.english_helper: Weed finally reaches the point where he can change class.

sentences.chinese: 很多人都很兴奋。
sentences.english_helper: Many people are excited.

sentences.chinese: 有人想做骑士。
sentences.english_helper: Some want to become knights.

sentences.chinese: 有人想做魔法师。
sentences.english_helper: Some want to become mages.

sentences.chinese: 大家都在说什么职业最强。
sentences.english_helper: Everyone is talking about which class is strongest.

sentences.chinese: 杂草没有马上说话。
sentences.english_helper: Weed does not speak right away.

sentences.chinese: 他站在一边，先看别人怎么选。
sentences.english_helper: He stands to the side and first watches how other people choose.

sentences.chinese: 后来，他听见有人提到雕刻师。
sentences.english_helper: Later, he hears someone mention sculptor.

sentences.chinese: 那个人一说完，旁边的人都笑了。
sentences.english_helper: As soon as that person finishes speaking, the people nearby all laugh.

sentences.chinese: 有人说：“那种职业能做什么？”
sentences.english_helper: Someone says, 'What can that class even do?'

sentences.chinese: 还有人说：“选那个，不如什么都不选。”
sentences.english_helper: Another person says, 'Choosing that is almost the same as choosing nothing.'

sentences.chinese: 杂草听见这些话，心里当然也动了一下。
sentences.english_helper: When Weed hears those words, his heart naturally wavers a little.

sentences.chinese: 他最怕的，不是难。
sentences.english_helper: What he fears most is not difficulty.

sentences.chinese: 他最怕的是，自己花了很久，最后还是没有钱，也没有路。
sentences.english_helper: What he fears most is spending a long time and ending with no money and no path.

sentences.chinese: 所以他的老想法又出来了。
sentences.english_helper: So his old way of thinking appears again.

sentences.chinese: 看起来强的，应该比较安全。
sentences.english_helper: What looks strong should be safer.

sentences.chinese: 看起来有名的，应该比较容易走。
sentences.english_helper: What looks famous should be easier to follow.

sentences.chinese: 可是他又想到一件事。
sentences.english_helper: But then he thinks of something else.

sentences.chinese: 真正有用的东西，不一定会在表面上写出来。
sentences.english_helper: Something truly useful is not always written on the surface.

sentences.chinese: 如果每个人都觉得一个职业没用，也许只是因为他们只看今天。
sentences.english_helper: If everyone thinks a class is useless, maybe it is only because they are looking at today.

sentences.chinese: 杂草却总是看得更长远。
sentences.english_helper: But Weed always tries to look further ahead.

sentences.chinese: 他慢慢走过去。
sentences.english_helper: He slowly walks over.

sentences.chinese: 周围的人还在把雕刻师当笑话。
sentences.english_helper: The people around are still treating sculptor as a joke.

sentences.chinese: 杂草听见了，可是没有回头。
sentences.english_helper: Weed hears them, but he does not turn back.

sentences.chinese: 他做出了自己的选择。
sentences.english_helper: He makes his own choice.

sentences.chinese: 那一刻，没有掌声。
sentences.english_helper: At that moment, there is no applause.

sentences.chinese: 也没有人觉得他很聪明。
sentences.english_helper: And no one thinks he is very clever.

sentences.chinese: 可是杂草心里反而安静。
sentences.english_helper: But inside, Weed actually feels calm.

sentences.chinese: 因为他终于不是照着别人的眼睛选路。
sentences.english_helper: Because he is finally not choosing his path by other people's eyes.

sentences.chinese: 他是在照着自己最清楚的地方走。
sentences.english_helper: He is following the part he understands most clearly himself.

stories.title: 第一把雕刻刀
stories.source_range: inspired by receiving the sculptor path and early tool scenes

sentences.chinese: 换了职业以后，杂草拿到了一把小小的雕刻刀。
sentences.english_helper: After changing class, Weed receives a small sculpting knife.

sentences.chinese: 那把刀不像剑。
sentences.english_helper: That knife does not look like a sword.

sentences.chinese: 也不像别人的高级武器。
sentences.english_helper: It also does not look like other people's high-level weapons.

sentences.chinese: 它很短，也很安静。
sentences.english_helper: It is short and quiet.

sentences.chinese: 杂草把它拿在手里，先没有说话。
sentences.english_helper: Weed holds it in his hand and says nothing at first.

sentences.chinese: 他本来以为，真正能改变人的东西，应该看起来更厉害一点。
sentences.english_helper: He had thought that something truly able to change a person should look more impressive.

sentences.chinese: 至少，也应该大一点。
sentences.english_helper: At least, it should be bigger.

sentences.chinese: 可是现在，他手里只有这一把小刀。
sentences.english_helper: But now, there is only this little knife in his hand.

sentences.chinese: 旁边的人看见了，又有人笑。
sentences.english_helper: People nearby see it, and some laugh again.

sentences.chinese: 有人说：“这也算武器吗？”
sentences.english_helper: Someone says, 'Does that even count as a weapon?'

sentences.chinese: 杂草这次没有理他们。
sentences.english_helper: This time, Weed does not pay attention to them.

sentences.chinese: 他只是低头看着刀。
sentences.english_helper: He only looks down at the knife.

sentences.chinese: 刀口很锋利。
sentences.english_helper: The edge is very sharp.

sentences.chinese: 他的手指刚碰上去，就马上收回来。
sentences.english_helper: As soon as his finger touches it, he pulls back at once.

sentences.chinese: 他忽然明白，这种东西不是用来乱挥的。
sentences.english_helper: He suddenly understands that this kind of thing is not for swinging around carelessly.

sentences.chinese: 这种东西要小心。
sentences.english_helper: This kind of thing needs care.

sentences.chinese: 要轻轻地用。
sentences.english_helper: It has to be used lightly.

sentences.chinese: 也要认真地看。
sentences.english_helper: And it has to be watched carefully.

sentences.chinese: 杂草以前一直相信，力气大一点，就会更快。
sentences.english_helper: Weed used to believe that more force would make things faster.

sentences.chinese: 可是这把刀给他的感觉完全不一样。
sentences.english_helper: But this knife gives him a completely different feeling.

sentences.chinese: 这把刀像是在说，快没有用。
sentences.english_helper: The knife seems to say that speed is useless here.

sentences.chinese: 乱也没有用。
sentences.english_helper: Being careless is useless too.

sentences.chinese: 如果真的想做出东西，就要先安静下来。
sentences.english_helper: If he truly wants to make something, he first has to become calm.

sentences.chinese: 杂草把刀收好。
sentences.english_helper: Weed puts the knife away carefully.

sentences.chinese: 这一次，他没有把它当成一个小东西。
sentences.english_helper: This time, he does not treat it as something small.

sentences.chinese: 他开始珍惜这把刀。
sentences.english_helper: He begins to treasure this knife.

sentences.chinese: 因为他觉得，也许真正大的改变，本来就不是从大东西开始。
sentences.english_helper: Because he feels that maybe truly big change does not begin with big things at all.

stories.title: 第一块木头
stories.source_range: inspired by early sculpting practice scenes

sentences.chinese: 杂草第一次正式练雕刻的时候，面前只放着一块普通木头。
sentences.english_helper: The first time Weed formally practices sculpting, there is only an ordinary piece of wood in front of him.

sentences.chinese: 那块木头一点也不特别。
sentences.english_helper: That piece of wood is not special at all.

sentences.chinese: 又小，又旧。
sentences.english_helper: It is small and worn.

sentences.chinese: 杂草一开始有点失望。
sentences.english_helper: At first, Weed feels a little disappointed.

sentences.chinese: 他本来以为，第一件东西至少应该让人一看就觉得厉害。
sentences.english_helper: He had thought that the first thing should at least look impressive at a glance.

sentences.chinese: 可是现在，他只好先低头做。
sentences.english_helper: But now, he can only lower his head and start working.

sentences.chinese: 第一刀下去的时候，他太快了。
sentences.english_helper: When the first cut goes down, he is too fast.

sentences.chinese: 木头马上歪了一边。
sentences.english_helper: The wood immediately goes crooked on one side.

sentences.chinese: 杂草看着那一块坏掉的地方，心里很不高兴。
sentences.english_helper: Weed looks at the damaged part and feels unhappy.

sentences.chinese: 他又试一次。
sentences.english_helper: He tries again.

sentences.chinese: 这一次慢一点。
sentences.english_helper: This time, he goes a little slower.

sentences.chinese: 可是线还是不直。
sentences.english_helper: But the line is still not straight.

sentences.chinese: 他本来以为，有决心就够了。
sentences.english_helper: He had thought that determination alone would be enough.

sentences.chinese: 现在他才发现，手也要跟上。
sentences.english_helper: Now he finds that his hands also have to catch up.

sentences.chinese: 眼睛也要跟上。
sentences.english_helper: His eyes also have to catch up.

sentences.chinese: 心也要跟上。
sentences.english_helper: And his mind also has to catch up.

sentences.chinese: 杂草坐了一会儿，没有马上再下刀。
sentences.english_helper: Weed sits for a while and does not cut again right away.

sentences.chinese: 他先看着那块木头。
sentences.english_helper: He first looks at the piece of wood.

sentences.chinese: 后来又看着自己手里的刀。
sentences.english_helper: Then he looks at the knife in his hand.

sentences.chinese: 最后，他轻轻地呼了一口气。
sentences.english_helper: Finally, he lets out a soft breath.

sentences.chinese: 这一次，他决定重新来。
sentences.english_helper: This time, he decides to start again.

sentences.chinese: 不是为了快。
sentences.english_helper: Not for speed.

sentences.chinese: 也不是为了马上做出很大的东西。
sentences.english_helper: And not to immediately make something grand.

sentences.chinese: 他只想先把一个小小的表情做对。
sentences.english_helper: He only wants to get one small expression right first.

sentences.chinese: 一点一点地，木头终于没有再乱掉。
sentences.english_helper: Bit by bit, the wood finally stops going wrong.

sentences.chinese: 做完以后，那东西还是很简单。
sentences.english_helper: After finishing, the thing is still very simple.

sentences.chinese: 也说不上多好看。
sentences.english_helper: You could not call it very beautiful.

sentences.chinese: 可是杂草看了很久。
sentences.english_helper: But Weed looks at it for a long time.

sentences.chinese: 这一次，他没有笑自己。
sentences.english_helper: This time, he does not laugh at himself.

sentences.chinese: 因为他第一次明白，不满意的时候，不一定要生气。
sentences.english_helper: Because for the first time, he understands that when you are unsatisfied, you do not always have to get angry.

sentences.chinese: 有时候，你只要安静地再做一次。
sentences.english_helper: Sometimes, you only need to quietly do it one more time.

stories.title: 没有笑的人
stories.source_range: inspired by early sculpting reactions and public judgment scenes

sentences.chinese: 杂草做出第一件还算像样的东西以后，把它带到了市场。
sentences.english_helper: After making his first passable piece, Weed brings it to the market.

sentences.chinese: 市场里有很多摊位。
sentences.english_helper: There are many stalls in the market.

sentences.chinese: 也有很多人在大声说话。
sentences.english_helper: There are also many people speaking loudly.

sentences.chinese: 有人卖武器。
sentences.english_helper: Some people are selling weapons.

sentences.chinese: 有人卖衣服。
sentences.english_helper: Some are selling clothes.

sentences.chinese: 杂草手里拿着自己的小雕刻，看起来很不起眼。
sentences.english_helper: Weed holds his small sculpture in his hand, and it does not stand out much.

sentences.chinese: 他一开始站得很直。
sentences.english_helper: At first he stands very straight.

sentences.chinese: 好像只要自己站得稳一点，别人就会觉得那东西也不错。
sentences.english_helper: As if standing more firmly would make others think the piece is good too.

sentences.chinese: 可是第一个看见的人笑了。
sentences.english_helper: But the first person who sees it laughs.

sentences.chinese: 第二个人也笑了。
sentences.english_helper: The second person also laughs.

sentences.chinese: 后来还有人直接取笑他。
sentences.english_helper: Later, someone directly mocks him.

sentences.chinese: 那个人说：“这个也有人要吗？”
sentences.english_helper: That person says, 'Would anyone even want this?'

sentences.chinese: 杂草听见以后，手一下子冷了。
sentences.english_helper: After hearing that, Weed's hand suddenly feels cold.

sentences.chinese: 他差一点就想把东西收起来。
sentences.english_helper: He almost wants to put the piece away.

sentences.chinese: 在他心里，一个老想法又出来了。
sentences.english_helper: In his heart, an old thought appears again.

sentences.chinese: 如果大部分人都笑，说明这东西一定没有用。
sentences.english_helper: If most people laugh, then this thing must be useless.

sentences.chinese: 可是就在这时候，一个老人走到了他面前。
sentences.english_helper: But at that moment, an old man walks up in front of him.

sentences.chinese: 老人没有笑。
sentences.english_helper: The old man does not laugh.

sentences.chinese: 老人只是低头看了很久。
sentences.english_helper: He only lowers his head and looks for a long time.

sentences.chinese: 后来，老人轻轻点头。
sentences.english_helper: Later, the old man nods lightly.

sentences.chinese: 老人说：“这东西还不成熟。
sentences.english_helper: The old man says, 'This piece is not mature yet.'

sentences.chinese: 可是做它的人很认真。”
sentences.english_helper: 'But the person who made it was serious.'

sentences.chinese: 杂草听见这句话，心里忽然一静。
sentences.english_helper: When Weed hears that sentence, his heart suddenly becomes quiet.

sentences.chinese: 因为老人看到的，不只是东西本身。
sentences.english_helper: Because the old man sees not only the object itself.

sentences.chinese: 老人也看见了他花在上面的时间。
sentences.english_helper: He also sees the time Weed put into it.

sentences.chinese: 看见了他没有放弃的样子。
sentences.english_helper: He sees the way Weed did not give up.

sentences.chinese: 杂草最后没有把那件东西丢掉。
sentences.english_helper: In the end, Weed does not throw the piece away.

sentences.chinese: 他反而把它收得更好。
sentences.english_helper: Instead, he puts it away more carefully.

sentences.chinese: 他终于明白，不是每个人的笑声都一样重要。
sentences.english_helper: He finally understands that not everyone's laughter is equally important.

sentences.chinese: 只要有一个人真的看见了，你做的事就值得继续。
sentences.english_helper: As long as one person truly sees it, what you are doing is worth continuing.
